text: This is the largest piece of Mars we've ever found on Earth. It's huge, it weighs 25 kilograms, that's 55 pounds, it's enormous. And it's special because it contains a rare glass created only under intense shockwave pressures. From something like an asteroid impact, we think this piece was ejected off Mars by an asteroid impact and sent hurtling toward us on Earth where it crashed into the Sahara Desert. But how do we know it's from Mars and not somewhere else? Because unlike the Moon, we haven't brought back physical samples from Mars. But we have sent spacecraft to land on Mars, analyze the rocks, and send data back. Which told us that Mars had this very unique chemical composition. And this rock is the perfect match. And someone just bought it for $5.3 million. I want to piece some Mars for more optimistic science and tech stories. Follow along.
